threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the 48 bus is just a quick hop to the station, not a designated ‘cross-town adventure’ at rush hour.
Caught a whiff of jerk chicken on the 59 last night. Turns out the guy next to me was bringing dinner home from a pop-up. Smelled like heaven.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Saw a group of kids trading Pokémon cards on a bench while a street performer juggled flaming torches—it clicked, this place is beautifully chaotic.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can grab a döner at 2 AM, then find a spontaneous rave in an abandoned factory. Only in Kreuzberg!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local if they grumble about the Muni delay while casually holding a burrito in the other hand, like it’s just another Tuesday.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a saxophonist on the 21 Hayes yesterday, playing 'Take Five' as we stopped at Market. The whole bus started clapping. Pure Muni magic.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think Gràcia's streets are simply charming. They miss the hidden murals and the real stories behind them—like the one about the cat lady on Carrer de Verdi.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They still stop to take photos of the street lamps—those quirky modernista designs are lost on most of us locals.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think you can find street parking easily on a Saturday. The locals know it’s like hunting for gold in these parts.
Caught a night bus to Brixton, and a guy tried to sell a broken phone as 'vintage'. Only in London do you find street hawkers at 3 AM.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I heard kids arguing about who gets to play on the broken swings at Körnerpark. It’s a small chaos that feels so right.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a club with a petting zoo next door. Dance with a goat or two after a night out, it's a real vibe!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a real local by how they navigate Muni—no hesitation at transfers. Tourists still clutching their maps like a lifeline.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Watched a guy juggle on the 38 Geary while a dog snoozed at his feet. Only in the Tenderloin do the absurd and the mundane ride together.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think Gràcia is all about the squares, but many locals prefer the quiet side streets where the real charm and hidden cafes are.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival when they’re still taking photos of the street signs. Locals just know to ignore them.
